description: Leadership principles for building trust, clarity, and high-performing teams.
ethical_guardrail: Use leadership rules to create safety, autonomy, and ethical performance—not control, coercion, or abuse of power.

rules.name: Psychological Safety
rules.principle: Teams perform best when people can speak up, ask questions, and fail without fear of punishment.
detection_triggers: People are afraid to speak up
Mistakes are punished
No one challenges bad ideas
Meetings are silent
The team hides problems

rules.name: Trust Equation
rules.principle: Trust increases with credibility, reliability, and intimacy, and decreases with self-orientation.
detection_triggers: They don't trust leadership
We need to rebuild trust
The relationship feels guarded
People think it's all politics
How do we increase credibility quickly?

rules.name: Situational Leadership
rules.principle: Match leadership style (direct, coach, support, delegate) to the follower’s competence and commitment.
detection_triggers: One style doesn't work for everyone
They need more support or autonomy
A new hire needs guidance
A senior person needs freedom
How do I adapt my leadership style?

rules.name: Delegation Ladder
rules.principle: Delegate by explicitly choosing the autonomy level, from 'do exactly' to 'own end-to-end'.
detection_triggers: I'm doing everything myself
Delegation keeps failing
They need clearer autonomy levels
I want them to own it, not just do tasks
How much control should I keep?

rules.name: Feedback Architecture
rules.principle: Effective feedback is specific, timely, behavior-focused, and tied to outcomes—not identity or character.
detection_triggers: Feedback conversations go badly
They get defensive with feedback
We need more frequent feedback
The same issue keeps repeating
How do I give feedback without demotivating?

rules.name: Vision Clarity
rules.principle: Clear direction aligns effort; people follow a direction they can see, repeat, and act on.
detection_triggers: People are confused about priorities
The team lacks direction
Everyone is busy but not aligned
We need a clear North Star
Why are efforts scattered?

rules.name: Leading vs Managing
rules.principle: Leading sets direction and meaning; managing controls execution and resources—both matter, but they are different jobs.
detection_triggers: I'm stuck in the weeds
I manage tasks but lack inspiration
We need better execution AND direction
Am I leading or just supervising?
The team needs motivation, not micromanagement

rules.name: Multiplier vs Diminisher
rules.principle: Multipliers amplify others’ intelligence and ownership; diminishers create dependency and drain capability.
detection_triggers: My team depends on me for everything
People feel drained after working with a leader
Some leaders create growth in others
I want to amplify my team's capabilities
We have talent but it's underused

rules.name: After-Action Review
rules.principle: Systematically review outcomes to capture lessons, prevent repeat mistakes, and improve the next iteration.
detection_triggers: We keep repeating the same mistakes
We finished a project but didn't learn
What went well/poorly?
We need a postmortem
How do we improve next time?

rules.name: Servant Leadership
rules.principle: Authority flows from service: remove obstacles, enable growth, and put the mission above ego.
detection_triggers: Leadership feels like control, not service
How can I support the team better?
I want authority without ego
Remove blockers for others
People follow leaders who help them
